questions.question: What is the GCF of $12$ and $18$?
A space probe travels $30$ miles in $5$ minutes. How many miles will it travel in $15$ minutes if it maintains the same GCF of speed?
What is the LCM of $8$ and $12$ using prime factorization?
A lab experiment requires $15$ ml of a chemical. If the chemical is only available in bottles of $3$ ml or $5$ ml, what is the LCM of the bottle sizes?
What is the GCF of $24$ and $30$ using factors?
A space station has two modules with $18$ and $24$ solar panels. What is the GCF of the number of solar panels?
A physics experiment involves a pendulum with a length of $12$ inches and a swing period of $8$ seconds. What is the LCM of the length and period?
What is the LCM of $9$ and $12$ using multiples?

A lab requires $12$ liters of a chemical to conduct an experiment. If the chemical is only available in bottles of $4$ liters or $6$ liters, determine the number of each type of bottle needed to obtain exactly $12$ liters using GCF and LCM.

A space mission requires a spacecraft to travel to two planets with orbital periods of $15$ days and $20$ days. Determine the LCM of the orbital periods and explain how this value is used to plan the mission.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of prime factorization in finding GCF and LCM.
Use real-world examples to illustrate the application of GCF and LCM in science and space exploration.
Encourage students to use diagrams and models to visualize the concepts of GCF and LCM.
